name: HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES
purpose: Authority registry for build-time and handoff-time gates that every Handshake product WP must demonstrate. Operationalizes interconnectivity, parallel agent + operator swarm safety, Argus visual + operator-GUI verification, quiet/non-intrusive operation, in-product UserManual currency, and UserManual diagnostic linkage. Does NOT duplicate refinement, enrichment, Codex, AGENTS, or role-protocol authority; cite-only relationships to those surfaces.

scope.applies_to: every WP that touches Handshake product code or product behavior under /src/, /app/, /tests/, or product runtime
exempt: pure repo-governance-only changes scoped per CX-111 HARD_GOVERNANCE_NO_WP_REQUIRED

enforcement.policy: On packet hydration, applicable HBR-* rules auto-generate PACKET_ACCEPTANCE_MATRIX rows keyed by rule_id. Validators must mark each row PROVED, NOT_APPLICABLE (with reason), or BLOCKED (with cause). PASS closure is illegal while any required HBR row is PENDING, STEER, or BLOCKED, per CX-503B1.
enforcement.implementation_owner: MT-001..MT-008 extended packet hydration tooling to read this registry and emit matching acceptance rows, wired hbr-matrix-check into gov-check, integrated validator-scan evidence verification, added the runtime handoff-gate evaluator, shipped HBR_VIOLATION typed receipts, and wired ProcessOwnershipLedger reclaim.

non_duplication_principle: Each HBR-* rule either operationalizes an existing rule (cited via citations array) or introduces a build-time obligation absent from refinement/enrichment/Codex/AGENTS/role-protocol surfaces. Restating existing authority is forbidden; surface drift if a citation goes stale.

non_authority_surfaces.note: No HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.md projection exists by default. Markdown is generated only on explicit operator request, per CX-205L, [GLOBAL-GOVARTIFACTS-001/002/003], and operator feedback feedback_no_default_md_files.

test_environment_prerequisites.note: Machine-local prerequisites the WP test suites require to run. EXPAND this list as new requirements appear, and INTEGRATE it into PostgreSQL provisioning / setup tooling / CI where needed so it does not stay prose-only (operator order 2026-06-18). A required extension that is absent MUST fail loud at migration/test time, never silently degrade a capability.

postgres_extensions.name: pgvector
postgres_extensions.required_for: WP-KERNEL-009 MT-264 LoomSearchV2 semantic + hybrid search (pgvector cosine HNSW kNN over 768-dim embeddings). Migration 0336 runs CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector and FAILS LOUD if absent.
postgres_extensions.install_note: NOT bundled with stock PostgreSQL. Built from source on this machine (MSVC BuildTools 2022, nmake /F Makefile.win against PGROOT) and installed as vector.dll + vector.control + vector--*.sql into C:/Program Files/PostgreSQL/16/{lib,share/extension}. Any other machine or CI runner executing the suite must provide pgvector >= 0.8.0.

postgres_extensions.name: pg_trgm
postgres_extensions.required_for: WP-KERNEL-009 MT-264 fuzzy/typo/substring search (GIN trigram) and general fuzzy matching.
postgres_extensions.install_note: Ships with PostgreSQL contrib; CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm.

postgres_extensions.name: pgcrypto
postgres_extensions.required_for: Pre-existing WP requirement (crypto/UUID helpers used by tests).
postgres_extensions.install_note: Ships with PostgreSQL contrib; CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pgcrypto.

test_environment_prerequisites.expansion_directive: When a future WP/MT introduces a new test-time prerequisite (DB extension, system library, toolchain component), add it here AND wire its ensure-present + fail-loud check into the migration/test path and any PG provisioning/setup script — do not leave the requirement implicit.

citation_kinds.external_reference: informational only, not Handshake authority (e.g., CKC source pattern)

pillars.name: Interconnectivity Proof
pillars.intent: Every WP touching a Handshake primitive must prove the wire to its neighbors at runtime, not just unit-test the primitive in isolation. Handshake's value is the orchestrated whole; lone passing units are insufficient evidence.

pillars.name: Parallel Agent + Operator Safety
pillars.intent: Handshake is built for parallel agent swarms working alongside the operator inside one app. Every WP must prove behavior under concurrent agent + operator load without contention, starvation, silent overwrites, or operator interruption.

pillars.name: Visual Debug + Operator GUI Drive
pillars.intent: Tests that pass without ever loading the operator surface are not evidence the operator or models can use the feature. Every observable WP drives the real GUI (or TraceProjection for non-UI work) and inspects the rendered result through Argus, the named headless/non-intrusive visual inspection and GUI steering path.

pillars.name: Non-Intrusive Operation
pillars.intent: Handshake runs alongside the operator working on something else. Automated runs, agent activity, sandbox work, and tests must not pop windows, steal focus, hijack keyboard, move the mouse, or leave orphan terminals.

pillars.name: In-Product UserManual Connectivity
pillars.intent: The swarm and the operator both must find wiring without chat history. Every changed Handshake behavior, tool, feature, primitive, workflow, model lane, diagnostic surface, storage/event contract, and operator/model navigation path is mirrored in the in-product internal UserManual; legacy ModelManual identifiers are aliases only; manual drift = build-rule fail.

pillars.name: Implementer Stop Discipline
pillars.intent: An implementer stop (claim-end, MT-end, WP-end, session-end, handoff) is a high-leverage failure point. Stops justified by token budget, session capacity, throughput, multi-session reasoning, or future-cost aggregates are the upstream cause of surface-shape implementations that pass tests without satisfying the spec. STOP rules forbid the motivational frame that produces deflection-shaped diffs; they are the upstream partner to INT (interconnectivity proof) and to the Spec-Realism Gate in role protocols.

rules.trigger: WP touches any product behavior

applicability.predicate_text: WP modifies any externally-observable product behavior in src/, app/, or product runtime surfaces

rules.required_action: Wire test proves the behavior emits a typed EventLedger append AND replay of the ledger reconstructs the behavior.
not_applicable_when: WP touches no externally-observable product behavior
EventLedger primitive not yet implemented at WP creation time

rules.trigger: WP touches any model invocation path

applicability.predicate_text: WP creates, modifies, or replaces any code path that calls an LLM or model adapter

rules.required_action: Wire test proves a stored ContextBundle is dispatched through ModelAdapter, tool requests pass through ToolGate, and model outputs land in ArtifactStore. No model invocation may bypass these primitives.
not_applicable_when: WP touches no model invocation surface

rules.trigger: WP produces any artifact intended for authority state

applicability.predicate_text: WP creates or modifies any path that mutates durable authoritative state

rules.required_action: Wire test proves the artifact reaches authority state ONLY via PromotionGate event; negative test proves direct mutation paths fail closed.
not_applicable_when: WP produces no authority-state artifact

rules.trigger: WP touches any collaborative state surface

applicability.predicate_text: WP modifies CRDT-backed editable workspace state, presence, or live document surfaces

rules.required_action: Wire test proves CRDT updates persist, replay correctly after reconnect, and promote through ValidationRunner before reaching authority state.
not_applicable_when: WP touches no CRDT surface
CRDT layer not yet implemented at WP creation time

rules.trigger: WP touches any model lane adapter (local / CLI bridge / BYOK API / manual handoff)

applicability.predicate_text: WP creates, modifies, or replaces a ModelAdapter implementation for any lane

rules.required_action: Wire test proves the lane normalizes into the same SessionRun event stream as every other lane; no lane-specific authority leak; the TraceProjection cannot tell which lane ran beyond a lane label.
not_applicable_when: WP touches no model lane adapter

rules.trigger: WP touches any role/agent decision, blocker, insight, error, or session boundary

applicability.predicate_text: WP creates or modifies any code path that emits role-level decisions, blockers, errors, insights, or session-open/close events

rules.required_action: Wire test proves a typed FEMS record is written with source link and retrieval policy; freeform prose logs are not sufficient. Repo-governance memory dual-write per feedback_dual_write_repo_memory.
not_applicable_when: WP touches no decision/blocker/insight/error/session-lifecycle surface
FEMS product memory not yet implemented (use repo-governance memory dual-write as bridge)

rules.trigger: WP touches any observable behavior

applicability.predicate_text: WP modifies any behavior an operator or model needs to inspect after the fact

rules.required_action: Wire test proves the behavior is reconstructable from the Operator Surface TraceProjection using durable state alone (no chat history, no terminal scrollback, no provider memory).
not_applicable_when: WP touches no observable behavior
Operator Surface TraceProjection not yet implemented

rules.trigger: WP creates or modifies any UUID generation site in product code

applicability.predicate_text: WP introduces, modifies, or replaces any call site that mints a new Uuid in product code under src/, app/, or tests/

rules.required_action: All new UUID mint sites MUST use time-ordered UUID v7 (`Uuid::now_v7()`), not v4. v4 is reserved for fixtures, hardcoded test literals, and third-party-driven derives. Cargo manifests touching the uuid crate must declare the `v7` feature; `v4` may remain only while legacy sites are being swept. Wire test asserts `uuid.get_version_num() == 7` on a representative mint path and that two sequential mints satisfy `a <= b` (time order).
not_applicable_when: WP touches no UUID generation site
Touched site is a hardcoded fixture literal (e.g., "00000000-..."), a `Uuid::nil()`, or a `Uuid::parse_str` parse-only path
Touched site is inside `.GOV/`, third-party vendored code, or generated code the WP does not own

rules.trigger: WP touches any observable runtime behavior (emits/ wires Flight Recorder / EventLedger observability)

applicability.predicate_text: WP introduces or modifies any observable runtime behavior (a behavior that emits Flight Recorder / EventLedger / TraceProjection observability, or any in-app runtime behavior whose health/freeze/crash matters)

rules.required_action: Wire test proves that any observable runtime behavior is wired across the THREE-TIER diagnostic model and the outcome per tier is recorded as build evidence (WIRED | NOT_APPLICABLE-with-reason | DEFERRED-with-reason): (1) FLIGHT RECORDER - the kept-as-is backend BUSINESS-EVENT ledger; confirm/repair the FR emit where the behavior is a business event (no schema re-open). (2) INTERNAL_DIAGNOSTICS - the Handshake-native INTERNAL self-diagnostics tool (panic hook, UI-thread heartbeat, frame-time, CPU/RSS/GPU counters, and the OPEN diagnostic-event API any feature can call); wire in-app runtime behavior here - this is the diagnostic role the Flight Recorder was intended to fill but never did. (3) PALMISTRY - the EXTERNAL out-of-process watcher that survives Handshake freezes/crashes/heavy-CPU; register a tracker where a behavior's liveness/freeze/crash is worth surviving-capture. internal_diagnostics (internal self-sense) + Palmistry (external observation) record NO project/sensitive data (typed allowlist; standard mechanism names kept - heartbeat/minidump/watchdog/ring-buffer). They SUPPLEMENT, never replace, the Flight Recorder.
not_applicable_when: WP introduces no observable runtime behavior

internal_diagnostics / Palmistry (WP-KERNEL-012) not yet shipped at WP creation time (record the consideration as DEFERRED-with-reason, never silently skipped); the WP-KERNEL-016 retrofit applies this rule retroactively once 015 ships

rules.trigger: WP touches any shared state surface

applicability.predicate_text: WP modifies code that holds, writes, or arbitrates state visible to more than one session or actor

rules.required_action: Test runs at least two concurrent agent sessions plus simulated operator edits against the touched state. Passes only when there is no silent last-writer-wins, no lease starvation, no deadlock, and all conflicts surface as deterministic conflict reports or revision rejections.
not_applicable_when: WP touches no state visible to more than one session/actor

rules.trigger: WP introduces or modifies any lease, lock, queue, scheduler, or loop counter

applicability.predicate_text: WP changes any concurrency primitive: lease, lock, queue, scheduler, backpressure mechanism, cancellation token, loop counter, retry policy

rules.required_action: Test proves leases expire correctly, stale sessions are recoverable, backpressure works under load, cancellation is honored within a bounded time, and loop counters prevent runaway.
not_applicable_when: WP touches no concurrency primitive

rules.trigger: WP touches any operator-facing surface

applicability.predicate_text: WP modifies any UI panel, control, route, or operator-facing capability

rules.required_action: Test proves the operator can keep editing CRDT-backed surfaces while an agent session is active on adjacent state. Presence and conflict state must be visible in the UI.
not_applicable_when: WP touches no operator-facing surface
Operator Surface MVP not yet implemented

rules.trigger: WP introduces any cross-agent message, handoff, or routing decision

applicability.predicate_text: WP creates or modifies any inter-actor message, handoff, routing, or notification surface

rules.required_action: Every cross-agent message carries typed routing fields (target_role, target_session, correlation_id, requires_ack, ack_for); narrative-only routing is forbidden. Test proves a receiving agent can act from typed fields without parsing prose.
not_applicable_when: WP introduces no inter-agent message or routing surface

rules.trigger: WP touches any UI, automation, capture, diagnostic, or operator-facing control surface

applicability.predicate_text: WP modifies any code path the operator or a model agent observes through the Operator Surface

rules.required_action: Verification drives and inspects the running Operator Surface through Argus: stable author_id targeting, headless/non-intrusive argus.inspect/argus.click/argus.set_value/argus.screenshot or egui_kittest/AccessKit evidence, rendered/layout state compared against the expected UI, and request-level agent_label when parallel clients share one binding token. Legacy primitive names list_widgets/click_widget/set_value/screenshot are compatibility aliases only. The legacy Tauri/WebView2/CDP path is not acceptable for the native app. Unit tests, process exit codes, and uninspected screenshots do not satisfy this rule.
not_applicable_when: WP touches no operator-observable surface
Operator Surface MVP not yet implemented (use TraceProjection per HBR-VIS-004)

rules.trigger: Any captured frame produced for HBR-VIS-001

applicability.predicate_text: Triggered alongside every HBR-VIS-001 capture

rules.required_action: Every captured frame must include a renderer console/runtime error scan. Uncaught errors during the test window fail the gate.
not_applicable_when: HBR-VIS-001 marked NOT_APPLICABLE for the same WP

rules.trigger: WP touches any feature visible at multiple viewports or states

applicability.predicate_text: WP modifies any UI surface that has more than one viewport size or state worth verifying

rules.required_action: Capture matrix instead of a single shot: normal viewport, at least one constrained viewport, and at least one edge state (empty, error, loading, multi-item).
not_applicable_when: WP feature has only one meaningful viewport/state
HBR-VIS-001 marked NOT_APPLICABLE

rules.trigger: WP touches a non-UI behavior

applicability.predicate_text: WP modifies backend, kernel, sandbox, or runtime behavior with no direct UI surface

rules.required_action: Visual verification = TraceProjection view of the run reconstructed from durable state. The projection must answer: what task / what context / what return / what tool calls / what artifact / what validation / what promotion.
not_applicable_when: WP has a direct UI surface (covered by HBR-VIS-001)
TraceProjection not yet implemented

rules.trigger: GUI surface lacks an Argus capture, identification, steering, or observation path

applicability.predicate_text: During verification, the role discovers a UI/operator/diagnostic surface that Argus cannot see, identify with a stable target, steer safely, or re-observe after steering

rules.required_action: Treat the missing Argus path as HBR-VIS technical debt. If the missing visibility/steering path blocks proof of the implemented surface, it is an allowed same-MT/WP scope increase: remediate the minimum stable author_id, snapshot state, steering hook, or headless capture path before claiming DONE/PASS. If the role cannot remediate within authority, record a blocking HBR-VIS gap with the exact surface, missing Argus capability, affected proof, and recommended remediation.
not_applicable_when: All in-scope GUI surfaces have automation paths

rules.trigger: Every automated test or agent run

applicability.predicate_text: Triggered for every WP that runs automated tests or governed agent activity

rules.required_action: Run Handshake in headless or hidden-window mode. The operator's foreground application stays in front. Pass requires zero focus/Z-order change events during the test window.
not_applicable_when: WP runs no automated tests or agent activity (rare; document why)

rules.trigger: Every automation surface

applicability.predicate_text: WP introduces or modifies any agent-facing or operator-facing automation surface

rules.required_action: Every automation surface must be reachable without OS-level keyboard injection, cursor movement, focus stealing, or foregrounding. Any code path requiring those mechanisms fails the gate. Negative test must prove the surface cannot be accidentally driven by global input hijacking.
not_applicable_when: WP introduces no automation surface

rules.trigger: WP launches background process, sandbox runner, scheduled job, or hidden CLI

applicability.predicate_text: WP introduces or modifies any process lifecycle: spawn, schedule, supervise, reclaim

rules.required_action: Every background launch records ownership metadata (owner_session, owner_wp, owner_role, started_at) and is reclaimed at session close, failure, staleness, or operator cancel. Test proves no blank terminal windows and no orphan processes after a run, failure, or kill.
not_applicable_when: WP launches no background or hidden processes

rules.trigger: WP requires foreground interaction that cannot be eliminated

applicability.predicate_text: WP genuinely needs OS-foreground interaction (e.g., capturing operator gesture, hardware-only input)

rules.required_action: Declare the foreground requirement in the WP before any run. Use a bounded controlled test window or documented manual step. Surface an operator-visible warning before launch. The default is forbidden; exceptions must be justified and time-bounded.
not_applicable_when: WP has no foreground interaction requirement (the common case)

rules.trigger: WP wires or modifies any model-callable command, tool, IPC channel, automation surface, operator-facing capability, documented product feature, feature primitive, workflow, diagnostic surface, storage/event contract, model lane, or operator/model navigation path

applicability.predicate_text: WP introduces, renames, removes, or changes the contract of any product surface, behavior, primitive, workflow, diagnostic surface, storage/event contract, or navigation path a no-context model or operator must discover

rules.required_action: Update the in-product internal UserManual in the same commit as the surface change. Legacy ModelManual identifiers are aliases only and MUST NOT create a parallel manual surface. Bump MANUAL_VERSION when applicable. Add/remove/rename the entry in the relevant feature group and commandReference/navigation reference.
not_applicable_when: WP wires or changes no Handshake product behavior, feature, primitive, workflow, tool, command, IPC, config, model lane, diagnostic surface, storage/event contract, operator-facing surface, model navigation path, or operator navigation path
UserManual artifact is not yet implemented at WP creation time; record DEFERRED-with-reason plus integration follow-up instead of silently skipping

rules.trigger: Any change to UserManual content

applicability.predicate_text: WP modifies the in-product internal UserManual artifact

rules.required_action: UserManual content must let a no-context capable model and the operator use Handshake (purpose, workflows, startup, commands, expected I/O, navigation, safety constraints, common failure modes, recovery steps) without reading repo files or chat history.
not_applicable_when: WP does not modify UserManual content

rules.trigger: Any UserManual entry that describes a wired code surface

applicability.predicate_text: Triggered for every UserManual entry that names a command, IPC channel, schema field, config key, CLI flag, route, backend navigation path, diagnostic surface, storage/event contract, or other code-defined surface

rules.required_action: UserManual entries must be code-truthful: a self-consistency test verifies each named surface exists at the named contract. Drift = build-rule fail.
not_applicable_when: Entries explicitly tagged as roadmap (not yet wired)

rules.trigger: Every implementation MT/WP that creates, changes, wires, exposes, deprecates, or removes a Handshake product behavior, tool, feature, primitive, workflow, model lane, command, IPC channel, config key, diagnostic surface, storage/event contract, operator navigation path, or model navigation path

applicability.predicate_text: Implementation changes anything future no-context models or the operator need to understand or use inside Handshake, including model orchestration, local/cloud/subagent lanes, backend navigation, diagnostics, storage/event authority, CRDT collaboration, visual/debug surfaces, or recovery workflows

rules.required_action: Update the in-product internal UserManual in the same implementation change so future no-context models and the operator know the feature purpose, usage path, startup/invocation commands when applicable, expected inputs and outputs, affected tools/features/primitives, failure modes, recovery steps, and verification/diagnostic proof. The manual entry must link the behavior to Flight Recorder/EventLedger evidence and record the HBR-INT-009 diagnostic posture for Flight Recorder, internal_diagnostics, and Palmistry as WIRED, NOT_APPLICABLE-with-reason, or DEFERRED-with-reason. If internal_diagnostics or Palmistry are not present in the current worktree, record DEFERRED-with-reason plus integration follow-up; never silently skip the linkage. Legacy ModelManual identifiers are aliases only and MUST NOT create a parallel manual surface.
not_applicable_when: Change is pure repo-governance work and touches no Handshake product behavior, tool, feature, primitive, workflow, model lane, command, IPC channel, config key, diagnostic surface, storage/event contract, operator navigation path, or model navigation path

rules.trigger: Implementer is about to estimate or reason about session capacity, tokens, throughput, or future-work aggregate cost

applicability.predicate_text: Every implementer role (kernel_builder, coder) at every step of work claim, plan, implementation, proof, and handoff

rules.required_action: Never estimate session capacity, tokens remaining, throughput, or aggregate cost of future work.

citations.anchor: Spec-Realism Gate (KERNEL_BUILDER, CODER, VALIDATOR, WP_VALIDATOR, INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR)

provenance.origin_incident: 27-MT NEEDS_REIMPLEMENTATION reopen on WP-KERNEL-004 driven by deflection patterns whose upstream cause was session-budget reasoning

rules.trigger: Implementer generates a stop reason that names tokens, sessions, capacity, throughput, multi-session, or future-work aggregate

applicability.predicate_text: Every stop event the implementer considers — claim-end, MT-end, WP-end, session-end, handoff

rules.required_action: Any stop reason that names tokens, sessions, capacity, throughput, multi-session, or future-work aggregate is invalid by definition. Override it and continue.

rules.trigger: Implementer considers stopping work on an MT, WP, or session

applicability.predicate_text: Every stop event the implementer considers — claim-end, MT-end, WP-end, session-end, handoff

rules.required_action: Stops are only valid if work is complete AND can be proven in actual runtime.

rules.trigger: Implementer encounters a dependency that blocks completion of the current work

applicability.predicate_text: Every work item where a dependency (toolchain, library, upstream MT, external resource) is missing or incomplete

rules.required_action: If work cannot be completed because of dependencies, work on the dependencies first, then return to the outstanding work.

provenance.origin_incident: 27-MT NEEDS_REIMPLEMENTATION reopen on WP-KERNEL-004; closes the BLOCKED_ON_DEPENDENCY exit that was previously treated as a valid stop

rules.trigger: Implementer encounters out-of-scope work needed to unblock the current work

applicability.predicate_text: Every work item where progressing the in-scope work requires touching code, files, or systems outside the declared scope

rules.required_action: If work is out of scope, perform it but only as far as needed to unblock the previous work. At end of task/WP/MT, self-evaluate why I went out of scope, what I touched, and ask the operator for a waiver — and when asking, the operator must be explicitly told the why and the full list of what I touched.

deferred_pillars.name: UI Control Discipline
deferred_pillars.intent: Slider/numeric control inspection, layout discipline, accessibility checks, reset affordances.
deferred_pillars.deferred_until_condition: Operator Surface MVP exists with stable UI primitives
deferred_pillars.reason: Premature to bind UI control law to primitives that do not yet exist

deferred_pillars.name: Sample Corpus Discipline
deferred_pillars.intent: Operator-maintained sample corpus for image, media, identity, prompt-card, or workflow verification.
deferred_pillars.deferred_until_condition: First creative module (Atelier-Lens-CKC Week 5) attaches and corpus shape is known
deferred_pillars.reason: Corpus shape and verification mode depend on the first creative module's data contracts

deferred_pillars.name: Cost & Token Budget Discipline
deferred_pillars.intent: Per-WP token budget visibility, runaway-loop cost gating, model-lane cost attribution.
deferred_pillars.reason: Operator has de-prioritized cost gating during build-reset; flag retained because parallel swarms x cloud lanes burn money fast (see memory: feedback_token_budget_lenient and project_wp_speed_priority)

provenance.driver: Operator request 2026-05-16 to create a Handshake-language build-rules authority modeled on CKC build_rules.md
operator_corrections: 2026-05-16: build rules must not duplicate refinement/enrichment scope; five pillars only (INT/SWARM/VIS/QUIET/MAN)
2026-05-16: no default md projection; JSON is authority
2026-05-17: add HBR-INT-008 UUID v7 default per operator direction; UUID v7 to keep being used throughout the app

version_log.summary: Initial 5-pillar rule set (INT/SWARM/VIS/QUIET/MAN), 23 active rules, 3 deferred pillars (UI/SMP/COST). JSON authority only; no md projection. Enforcement mechanism = PACKET_ACCEPTANCE_MATRIX_EXTENSION, wiring deferred to follow-up RGF.

Add HBR-INT-008 (UUID v7 default): new UUID mint sites in product code MUST use time-ordered Uuid::now_v7(); v4 reserved for fixtures and third-party derives. Recorded under INT pillar because time-ordering preserves cross-primitive correlation/replay locality (EventLedger, snapshots, ContextBundle, ArtifactStore). Operator-driven addition during WP-KERNEL-003 close-out; KB003 product code sweeps to v7 in the same lane.

Enforcement wiring active per MT-001..MT-008. PACKET_ACCEPTANCE_MATRIX auto-emission, gov-check sub-check, validator-scan integration, handoff-gate evaluator, ProcessOwnershipLedger reclaim, and HBR_VIOLATION typed receipts all online.

version_log.change: Added STOP pillar + 5 HBR-STOP-* rules (OPERATOR-authored 2026-05-20) forbidding capacity/token/throughput reasoning as stop justification, requiring runtime-proof for valid stops, requiring dependency-first work over BLOCKED exits, and requiring full-disclosure waiver requests for scope expansion. Origin: 27-MT NEEDS_REIMPLEMENTATION reopen on WP-KERNEL-004.

Added test_environment_prerequisites registry (OPERATOR order 2026-06-18): pgvector >= 0.8.0 is REQUIRED for the test suite (WP-KERNEL-009 MT-264 LoomSearchV2 semantic/hybrid search; migration 0336 fails loud if absent), alongside pg_trgm and pgcrypto. Includes an expansion_directive to grow the list and integrate ensure-present + fail-loud checks into migrations/test paths/PG provisioning where needed, rather than leaving prerequisites implicit.

Added HBR-INT-009 (OPERATOR directive 2026-06-27): the THREE-TIER DIAGNOSTIC WIRING mandate. Every observable runtime behavior MUST be wired across three tiers with the per-tier outcome (WIRED / NOT_APPLICABLE-with-reason / DEFERRED) recorded as build evidence: (1) Flight Recorder = the kept-as-is backend BUSINESS-EVENT ledger (no schema re-open); (2) INTERNAL_DIAGNOSTICS = the NEW Handshake-native INTERNAL self-diagnostics tool (panic/heartbeat/frame-time/resource + open diagnostic-event API) - the diagnostic role the FR was intended to fill but never did; (3) PALMISTRY = the NEW EXTERNAL out-of-process watcher that survives freezes/crashes. internal_diagnostics + Palmistry record no project/sensitive data and keep standard mechanism names (heartbeat/minidump/watchdog). Motivated by a live 2026-06-26 UI-thread freeze the in-process FR was blind to. internal_diagnostics + Palmistry are built by WP-KERNEL-012; WP-KERNEL-016 retrofits this rule onto all already-shipped work. Cites HBR-INT-001/007 + Codex CX-006-VIS + the planned CX-981 HARD block. Canonical authority; the 6 role protocols project this via their Diagnostics duty bullets.

Added HBR-MAN-004 (OPERATOR directive 2026-06-28): every implementation that changes Handshake product behavior, tools, features, primitives, workflows, model lanes, diagnostics, storage/event contracts, commands/IPCs/config, or operator/model navigation must update the in-product internal UserManual in the same change. UserManual entries must explain no-context usage and connect behavior to Flight Recorder/EventLedger plus HBR-INT-009 Flight Recorder/internal_diagnostics/Palmistry posture. Palmistry/internal_diagnostics absence in the current worktree is DEFERRED-with-reason plus follow-up, never silent skip. Updated MAN-family wording from legacy ModelManual to UserManual with ModelManual retained only as an alias.

Added Argus as the named HBR-VIS visual inspection and GUI steering path (OPERATOR directive 2026-06-28), then updated it after MT-007 introduced the native Rust Argus MCP facade: UI/operator/diagnostic/frontend navigation/layout/style changes must be inspected through headless, non-intrusive Argus evidence before DONE/PASS. HBR-VIS-001 now requires stable author_id targeting plus argus.inspect/argus.click/argus.set_value/argus.screenshot or egui_kittest/AccessKit evidence, with list_widgets/click_widget/set_value/screenshot retained only as compatibility aliases. HBR-VIS-005 treats any in-scope surface Argus cannot see, identify, steer, or re-observe as HBR-VIS technical debt and an allowed same-MT/WP scope increase when it blocks proof. Canonical protocol: .GOV/roles_shared/docs/ARGUS_VISUAL_INSPECTION_PROTOCOL.md; Codex anchor: CX-503D1.
